conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you have the main responsibility for her day-to-day care (such as feeding, clothing, and making decisions about her upbringing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for her day-to-day care - I look after her needs, make sure she's fed and clothed, and make all the decisions about her upbringing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Do you live in the UK, and are you either a UK citizen, have settled status, or have the right to reside in the UK (such as through EU settled status, refugee status, or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm allowed to claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (This would be relevant for children aged 16-19, but I want to confirm her current educational status)

Also, does Alex live with anyone else who might also be responsible for her, or does she live solely with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, she's only 8, so she's just in primary school - nothing like college or training courses. And no, she lives just with me, nobody else is responsible for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Let me ask one final question to complete the assessment:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period, or does she live with you at home full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that - she lives with me at home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit** for your daughter Alex.

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- She lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for her day-to-day care
- You live in the UK and are allowed to claim benefits
- Alex lives solely with you and is not in hospital or local authority care

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can apply for Child Benefit and should receive payments for Alex.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, but you would still be eligible to claim Child Benefit itself.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) She is under 16 years old (she is 8); (2) She lives in the UK with the claimant; (3) The claimant is responsible for Alex, providing her day-to-day care including feeding, clothing, and making decisions about her upbringing; (4) The claimant lives in the UK and is allowed to claim benefits; (5) Alex lives solely with the claimant and no one else is responsible for her; (6) Alex is not in hospital or local authority care for an extended period - she lives at home full-time. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one daughter, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them in the UK. The claimant has main responsibility for Alex's day-to-day care and is eligible to claim benefits in the UK. Alex is under 16, not in hospital or care, and lives at home full-time with only the claimant responsible for her. All Child Benefit eligibility criteria are satisfied.
